data.title: ADT prices 71M shares secondary offering, announces concurrent share repurchase
data.description:
• ADT (NYSE:ADT) priced the previously announced secondary public offering of 71M shares of the company’s common stock held by certain entities managed by affiliates of Apollo Global Management.
  • The underwriters will have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 10.65M shares.
  • The company also authorized the concurrent purchase from the underwriters at a price per share equal to the price per share to be paid by the underwriters. The share repurchase is part of the company’s existing $500M share repurchase program.
  • The company is not selling any shares and will not receive any proceeds from the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on July 28, 2025.
  • The stock price dropped 3.3% on Friday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Amazon abandons plans to build Irish plant due to power woes - report
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has abandoned plans to build an industrial facility in Dublin after it could not secure power supply for the €300M project, The Irish Times reported.

The proposed plant would have employed about 500 people. The facility was to be set up at Ballycoolin in northwest Dublin, near the Co Meath border, where Amazon already has a large base, the report added.

Amazon did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Amazon's cloud computing unit Amazon Web Services, or AWS, wanted the site for making high-tech server racks, a form of shelving used in data centers that drive AI technology, but now it will not go ahead because of constraints in power networks in the area, the report noted.

However, failure to provide Amazon with electricity surprised people with knowledge of the plan because prospective power demand from the proposed facility, which was not a data center, was considered relatively modest, the report added.

Amazon stopped the AWS investment when ESB Networks noted that there was no scope to provide an electricity connection within the company’s timeframe due to constraints in power networks in that area, according to the report.

“AWS was disappointed that it did not prove possible to proceed with this project. If conditions allow, we do hope to be able to make other high-tech investments elsewhere in Ireland,” said Amazon to the news outlet.

data.title: Stock index futures tick up day after S&P 500, Nasdaq touch fresh closing highs
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked higher on Friday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite hit fresh record high closes. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis point to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) were flat at 3.93%.

Wall Street expressed a cautious tone on Thursday, with the major averages closing mixed as investors weighed a wave of corporate earnings—highlighted by results from Alphabet and Tesla—against rising Treasury yields following an unexpected fall in jobless claims.

"The risk-on tone just about continued yesterday, as another batch of strong U.S. data supported investor optimism, with the S&P 500 ending a quiet session at a 4th consecutive all-time high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

On the earnings end, Intel (INTC) shares were down 5% in premarket trade after the company's bottom line in the second quarter missed Wall Street estimates. The firm's Q3 outlook also failed to impress investors. The company also announced a 15% workforce reduction. 

Phillips 66 (PSX) will release its quarterly results on Friday during market hours. 

The economic calendar is also on the lighter side, with mainly the June durable goods orders report due before the opening bell.

Core durable goods orders is expected to come in at 0.1% M/M, and durable goods orders at -10.4% M/M.

"June durable goods orders data will not clarify the U.S. monetary policy outlook. The Biden factory building boom supported some equipment orders, but that boom has stagnated this year. Democrat consumers showed signs of front-loading durable goods purchases ahead of tariffs, but Republicans did not. The numbers end up being noisy," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Europe indexes turn red as investors read corporate results and await U.S. EU deal
data.description:
London (UKX:IND) -0.31% to 9,109, UK retail sales rise lower than expected in June.

The British pound fell to $1.347, retreating further from its two-week high of $1.358

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.84% to 24,091. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.48% to 7,781. Consumer confidence in France edged up to 89 in July.

The unemployment rate in Hungary rose to 4.5% in the April–June.

Sweden’s unemployment rate stood at 9.4% in June. Producer prices in Sweden fell 3.1% Y/Y in June.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.58% lower to 548.4, as investors weighed a flurry of corporate earnings, the ECB's latest policy decision, and ongoing EU-U.S. trade discussions. U.S. and EU officials are reportedly edging closer to a trade agreement that would introduce a 15% tariff on most European goods.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.41%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 6 basis points to 2.76%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.66%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)
